commit.message:
Add montage video player with automatic active song sync from timestamps (#17)

Extracts the montage video and embedded player from PR #16, with the key
addition of automatic active song synchronization based on video
timestamps. When the montage plays on the dashboard, `activeSong`
updates via `useSyncedState` so all connected voters see the current
song highlighted in real time on their vote page.

### Constants & data model
- `DEFAULT_ESC_YEAR` (2026) and `ESC_MONTAGE_DATA` (year-keyed map
bundling YouTube video ID + timestamp entries) in `constants.ts`
- `escYear` added to `Game` interface, migration 004, `createGame`
(defaults to `DEFAULT_ESC_YEAR`), and `getGameByToken`
- Montage YouTube ID and timestamps are derived from the year at runtime
via `ESC_MONTAGE_DATA[escYear]`, ensuring they stay in sync and
future-proofing for other ESC years

### Montage player (`montage-player.tsx`)
- Embeds `react-player@3.4.0` (v3 — HTML5 video element API, not v2's
`url`/`onProgress`)
- Receives `timestamps` as a prop (decoupled from global constants)
- On `timeupdate`, maps `currentTime` → country via linear lookup over
the provided timestamps
- Calls `onSongChange` only when the country actually changes, avoiding
unnecessary synced state writes

```tsx
// Dashboard wires the player's song changes to useSyncedState
<MontagePlayer
  youtubeId={montageData.youtubeId}
  timestamps={montageData.timestamps}
  onSongChange={(country) => setActiveSong(country)}
  onEnded={() => { setIsMontageActive(false); setActiveSong(null); }}
/>
```

### Dashboard controls
- "▶️ Play Montage" / "⏹ Stop Montage" toggle (visible when
`ESC_MONTAGE_DATA` has an entry for the game's `escYear`)
- Clicking a manual song button while montage is active stops the
montage
- Stop button clears both montage and active song state

### Not included from PR #16
TV Display page (`/:token/tv`), remote control features (`tvPlayback`,
`tvProgress`, `tvMode` synced state channels) — left for future.

---------

commit.message:
fix: EPay mapi.php API-first with submit.php fallback

Strategy: try mapi.php server-to-server first, fall back to submit.php redirect.
mapi.php returns the actual payment gateway URL (alipay/wxpay), so the
user's browser never touches the EPay CDN — avoids region blocking (403).

Also:
- Auto-normalize api_url: strip trailing /submit.php, /mapi.php
- Disable redirect following for mapi requests
- 10s timeout for mapi requests
- Detailed error logging for mapi failures

fix: add contents:write permission for release upload

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

scripts/main: add 7 new web components and UIToastManager global API

Shadow DOM components:

UIToast (<ui-toast>)
  Attributes: variant (info/success/warning/danger), message, duration (ms).
  Renders inside shadow root with scoped toast-in / toast-out keyframe
  animations (translateX slide from right). Auto-dismisses after duration ms;
  manual dismiss via close button adds .is-dismissing class then removes element.
  Role=alert for danger, role=status for others; aria-live assertive/polite.

UIDialog (<ui-dialog>)
  Attributes: open (boolean), title.
  Shadow root renders full-screen backdrop (color-mix overlay, backdrop-filter
  blur) + centred dialog card with header/body/footer slots. Clicking backdrop
  closes; Esc key closes via document keydown listener added/removed in
  attributeChangedCallback. Dispatches ui-open / ui-close CustomEvents.
  aria-modal=true, aria-labelledby wired to title h2. Body overflow:hidden while
  open. open() / close() methods exposed for imperative control.

Light DOM components (inherit page CSS tokens and .badge / .breadcrumb rules):

UIQuantity (<ui-quantity>)
  Attributes: value, min, max, step, name, disabled.
  Renders .quantity-stepper div with − button, number input, + button into
  light DOM so the input participates in ancestor forms natively (no
  ElementInternals needed). Buttons disabled at min/max bounds; input clamped
  on change. Dispatches 'change' CustomEvent with detail.value.

UIBadge (<ui-badge>)
  Attributes: variant (default/success/warning/danger/info/accent), label.
  Sets className to 'badge badge--<variant>' on the host element; optionally
  sets textContent from label attr. Reuses all existing .badge CSS rules.

UIBreadcrumb (<ui-breadcrumb>)
  Attribute: items (JSON array of {label, href?}).
  Renders <nav aria-label=Breadcrumb><ol> into light DOM; last item gets
  .breadcrumb__item--current + aria-current=page; separators via CSS ::after.

UIPrice (<ui-price>)
  Attributes: amount (float), currency (ISO 4217, default USD), original (float),
  size. Formats with Intl.NumberFormat in 'currency' style. Renders
  .price__current span; if original > amount also renders <del>.price__original
  for strikethrough discount display. className set to 'price price--<size>'.

UIToastManager (window.UIToast)
  Singleton object lazily creating .ui-toast-container fixed div appended to
  body. API: UIToast.show(msg, variant, duration), plus .success/.danger/
  .warning/.info shorthand methods. Each call creates and appends a <ui-toast>.

All 7 elements registered with customElements.define guarded by .get() checks.

commit.message:
Implement MVP authentication with Laravel Sanctum (#43)

Adds token-based API authentication aligned to Laravel Sanctum as the
minimal auth layer required before protected social endpoints (follows,
RSVPs, check-ins) can be built.

## Auth endpoints (`/api/v1/auth/`)

| Method | Path | Auth required |
|--------|------|---------------|
| `POST` | `/register` | — |
| `POST` | `/login` | — |
| `POST` | `/logout` | ✓ |
| `GET` | `/me` | ✓ |

`register` and `login` return a plain-text Sanctum token:

```json
{
  "token": "1|abc123...",
  "user": { "id": 1, "name": "Jane Doe", "email": "jane@example.com" }
}
```

Protected routes use `Authorization: ******

## Infrastructure

- `laravel/sanctum ^4.3` added; `personal_access_tokens` migration
published
- `HasApiTokens` trait added to `User` model
- Placeholder `auth:sanctum`-guarded `social` route group added for
future social endpoints
- `SecurityScheme` (Bearer) and `Auth` tag added to OpenAPI annotations
- `SANCTUM_STATEFUL_DOMAINS` added to `.env.example`

commit.message:
refactor: extract agent panel UI and move autonomy into Rust hooks (#92)

## Summary

This branch finishes two cross-cutting cleanups that now need to land
together: the agent-panel extraction work that moves more presentational
UI into shared packages, and the ACP permission-autonomy refactor that
moves auto-approval into backend Rust hook points instead of
provider-owned execution profiles or frontend fallbacks.

## What changed
- extracted more of the agent panel scene/composer surface into shared
UI and contract packages, tightened the website demo around the real
desktop model, and added guard rails for the scene MVC split
- moved autonomous permission approval into Rust-owned session policy
hooks for Claude, Codex native, and subprocess ACP providers, including
canonical `autoAccepted` projection state and backend session policy
storage
- deleted the old execution-profile plumbing and frontend auto-accept
fallback so reconnect, resume, and permission rendering now follow the
backend-owned autonomy model
- closed the retroactive review findings by clearing build-only autonomy
when sessions enter `plan`, only projecting auto-approved subprocess
permissions after the response write succeeds, and short-circuiting
Claude’s later `PermissionRequest` callback under autonomy
- documented the autonomy architecture in
`docs/solutions/best-practices/autonomous-mode-as-rust-side-policy-hook-2026-04-11.md`

## Validation
- focused Rust permission-path regressions for client loop and Claude
permission hooks
- `AGENT=1 bun test
src/lib/acp/store/services/session-connection-manager.test.ts`
- `bun run check` still reports the known agent-panel/parity baseline
failures in `desktop-agent-panel-scene*` and
`packages/ui/src/components/agent-panel/index.ts`

commit.message:
fix: add mergedPRDates for accurate chart filter breakdowns

The Top Repositories chart was showing identical bar values across all
filter periods (30d / 90d / year) because pullRequestDetails only stores
the last 10 PRs per repo. For active repos all 10 fall within the last
30 days, so every time window produced the same counts.

Fix: add a cheap collectMergedPRDates() function that fetches the last
100 merged_at timestamps per repo in a single pulls.list() API call
(no expensive per-PR detail fetches). These are stored as RepoMetrics
.mergedPRDates and used by the chart filter in build-pages.ts instead
of the sparse pullRequestDetails. The existing pullRequestDetails (10
full-detail entries) are preserved unchanged for the PR table.

Backward-compatible: mergedPRDates is optional; old cached data falls
back to pullRequestDetails.

Fix snapshot/incremental race: move snapshot to feed thread

Snapshot delivery now happens synchronously in HandleSubscribe on the
feed thread, before activating incremental forwarding. This guarantees
correct ordering by construction:

  1. SubscribeOk → 2. BookSnapshot + InfoSnapshot → 3. Incrementals

Since the feed thread has exclusive access to the books, no seqlock or
buffering is needed. The cost (~1-5µs per subscribe) only occurs on
client subscription requests, not on the hot event path.

Removed: snapshot thread, seqlock reads, SnapshotRequest queue, IDisposable.

feat: unified implement lifecycle with review+fix loop (#60)

## Summary

Replaces the two-workflow model (`implement.yaml` +
`implement-fix.yaml`) with a single orchestrator that handles the full
implement → review → fix lifecycle internally.

Closes #54.

## Architecture

```
/implement (issue_number)
  └─ implement_issue()
       ├─ 1. CLI session A: implement the issue
       ├─ 2. commit, push, create PR
       ├─ 3. review_pr() — posts review on PR
       ├─ 4. If APPROVE → done
       │    If REQUEST_CHANGES:
       │         ├─ 5. get_unresolved_threads()
       │         ├─ 6. CLI --resume=A: fix with review context
       │         ├─ 7. commit, push
       │         └─ 8. goto 3 (up to 3 iterations)
       └─ 9. Return result with accumulated stats
```

## Changes

### New capabilities
- **Session ID parsing** — `_parse_session_id()` extracts UUID from CLI
output/transcript
- **Session resumption** — `run_copilot(session_id=...)` adds `--resume`
flag
- **Review+fix loop** — `implement_issue()` runs up to 3 review/fix
iterations
- **Graceful degradation** — handles no session ID, no unresolved
threads, no changes after fix, max iterations exhausted

### Removed
- `fix_pr()` — replaced by internal loop
- `/fix` endpoint, `FixRequest`, `_run_fix()` — no longer needed
- `implement-fix.yaml` workflow — the agent handles this internally now
- `FIX_PROMPT_TEMPLATE` (old version that included PR body)

### Updated
- `review_pr()` returns `original_event` for lifecycle decisions
- `_task_status_label()` handles `max_iterations` as partial
- 86 tests (5 new lifecycle tests, 6 removed fix tests)

release: 0.3.0 (#57)

* chore: bump project version to 0.3.0 and update User-Agent strings

* feat: add alembic_version column expansion script and integrate into startup routines

* refactor: add license header, improve error handling, and refine formatting in fix_alembic.py

* feat: implement asynchronous polling for vision metadata extraction in scanner components and update E2E tests

* ✔ feat: expand ontology to support video and board game media (#61)

* feat: implement database schema separation and telemetry hardening

* docs: update implementator workflow to require human intervention for PR creation and review requests

* feat: address PR #58 comments - docstrings, telemetry log, and migration hardening

* feat: implement centralized task executor and automated backups

* feat: add configuration variables for scheduled backups to .env.example

* Update .env.example

* docs: update implementator workflow to clarify PR submission instructions

* feat: implement manual entry form component and update acquisition workflow tests

* feat(scanner): address PR review feedback and harden manual entry

* docs: add post-push interaction step to pr-finalization workflow

* refactor: decouple server and browser API URL resolution logic in resolveApiUrl

* feat: implement video and board game ontologies with associated database models and schema migrations

* refactor: reformat migration script with consistent quoting and add license header

* feat: harden models, gate scheduler, and finalize ontology expansion

* feat: PR #61 finalization - ontology hardening, scheduler gating, and telemetry fixes

* ✔ refactor: apply code formatting and integrate smart image cropping in… (#63)

* refactor: apply code formatting and integrate smart image cropping into the vision extraction pipeline

* fix: handle mime_type after smart crop and improve tests stability

* feat: transition cover metadata extraction to an asynchronous task-based architecture and improve image orientation handling.

* test: update viewfinder SVG accessibility test to assert role presentation instead of aria-hidden

* refactor: replace manual camera stream capture with native file picker integration in Snap Cover workflow

* feat: inject Flask application context into background tasks and ensure upload state reset on camera capture failure

* refactor: improve cover processing with robust contour detection, automated cropping, and enhanced UI polling states.

* Update tasks.py formatting

* formatting

* Update snap_cover_error.test.ts 

Removed the strict class check. sonner toasts do not attach the text-destructive class to the text div. The toBeVisible() check sufficiently verifies the toast rendering.

* Update snap_cover_error.test.ts

Removed obsolete button clicks. Replaced them with a directpage.setInputFiles(' input[type="file"]',...) call to bypass UI differences between camera/desktop modes. Removed the invalid. toHaveClass check.

* feat: removing smart covers

* fix: removing smart_crop_and_warp

* fixed linting

* Steps 6-8: Games (#64)

* jigsaw base impl (#65)

* jigsaw base impl

* copyrights

* feat: add jigsaw puzzle ingestion support with UPCitemdb integration and scanner UI updates

* refactor: centralize media metadata and format definitions into a new registry for consistent UI and backend handling

* feat: implement MediaCategory and MediaFormat ontology definitions with cross-subsystem contract tests

* feat: optimize scanner top-bar for mobile responsiveness and update image remote patterns in next.config

* refactor: move flashlight toggle control from BottomSheet to TopBar and improve torch capability detection

* addressing failures and comments

* fixes

* fixed tests

* fix: address PR#57 review comments and complete v0.3.0 features

- Thread safety: Add threading.Lock + TTL eviction for task results
- Task ownership: Add user_id verification to prevent cross-user polling
- Camera-capture: Fail immediately on server errors (500/503) vs retry
- TMDB/BGG: Only store real barcodes, not title queries
- Directors: Use WorkContribution (CreationEvent) per FRBRoo ontology
- UPC: Fix IndexError on empty images array
- License: Add missing AGPL header line to llm_covers.py
- Test: Remove unused Item import
- Changelog: Remove OpenCV claims (not implemented)
- E2E: Add boardgame_workflow.test.ts for BGG ingestion
- Scanner: Add player count and runtime display for video/board games
- FTS: Include search_vector for Cast/Directors/Mechanics search

* fix: task ownership verification for tests and production

- Pass user_id through task wrapper to preserve ownership
- Fix ownership comparison to handle UUID vs string comparison
- Update test to mock the actual extraction function to avoid async issues
- Frontend lint passes, backend tests pass (345 passed)

* fix: update boardgame E2E test to use barcode format

- Use format=boardgame instead of format=game in route matcher
- Use Title key instead of title in mock API response
- Change title search test to use barcode for reliable matching
- All 11 E2E tests now pass

* fix: add min_players, max_players, runtime to IsbnMeta type

- Add min_players, max_players, minPlayers, maxPlayers for board games
- Add runtime, Runtime for video media
- Fix TypeScript error in success-card.tsx

* test: add success-card tests for board game and video metadata

- Test board game player count display
- Test video metadata with directors, cast, and runtime
- Would have caught missing type definition earlier

* fix: cast extendedMeta values to prevent TypeScript error

* fix: issues in 0.3.0 (#68)

* feat: wrap stats cards in links for direct collection filtering

* feat: change default collection sort order to updated and add sorting logic for updated and added timestamps

* fix: improve torch compatibility by forcing constraint application and adding a delay for capability detection

* feat: add cover image support and update layout for horizontal item card variant

* docs: add documentation for scheduled background backups and configuration options

* feat: implement optional_auth decorator and update manifestation endpoints to support conditional ownership status

* docs: add computer vision and networking dependencies to README tech stack

* chore: update project standards, refine virtual environment testing instructions, and finalize 0.3.0 changelog entries

* feat: add BGG API authentication, expand metadata fields, and include mandatory attribution component

* feat: improve TMDB metadata resolution via UPC title lookup and add mandatory TMDB attribution component

* feat: implement UPC lookup waterfall with Allegro integration

* test: add unit tests for Allegro API and clean up imports

* refactor: decouple TMDB from UPC waterfall resolution

* docs: enhance README and INSTALL documentation with external links, reorganized guides, and updated setup instructions.

* feat: support manual text searches in barcode lookup and add title-based video lookup test case

* feat: add Allegro user-context authentication, expand barcode ingestion logic, and improve metadata mapping.

* fix: lint and test issues in allegro scripts and tests

* fix: resolve remaining lint issues in backend and frontend

* fix: add copyright headers to remaining files

* fix: address Copilot review comments - improve token parsing, fix headers, and clean up

* Update CHANGELOG.md
